title: Vaporizable material handling for electronic vapor device
abstract: An electronic vapor device is disclosed comprising a first container for storing a vaporizable material, a vaporizer component coupled to the first container, configured for vaporizing the vaporizable material, a processor coupled to the vaporizer component, configured to control the vaporizer component in response to a disposition signal, and a network access device, coupled to the processor, configured for receiving the disposition signal from a remote server.
claims:
Claims 1. An electronic vapor device comprising: a first container for storing a vaporizable material; a vaporizer component coupled to the first container, configured for vaporizing the vaporizable material; a processor coupled to the vaporizer component, configured to control the vaporizer component in response to a disposition signal; and a network access device, coupled to the processor, configured for receiving the disposition signal from a remote server. 2. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, wherein the disposition signal comprises one or more of, a command to vaporize the vaporizable material, a command to disable the vaporizer component, a command to enable the vaporizer component, a command to place the vaporizer component in an on-demand state, a command to mix the vaporizable material with another vaporizable material, a command to mix the vaporizable material with a non-vaporizable material, a command to replenish the vaporizable material, a command to deplete the vaporizable material without vaporizing the vaporizable material. 3. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, wherein the network access device communicates via one or more of, Wi-Fi, TCP/IP, Ethernet, UDP, WAP, Bluetooth, Near Field Communication (NFC), Z-wave, LPWAP, Telnet, HTTP, HTTPs, GSM, CDMA, or LTE. 4. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, further comprising a disposition control mechanism operatively interposed between the vaporizer component and the first container, configured to execute a function based on the disposition signal 5. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, wherein the disposition control mechanism comprises at least one of: a valve, a membrane, a foil barrier, a stationary wick, a movable wick, a bubble jet, a piezoelectric pump, a microfluidic pump, a mechanical pump, or a heater. 6. The electronic vapor device of claim 5, wherein the disposition signal comprises a command to replenish the vaporizable material and wherein the disposition 

 control mechanism is configured to replenish the first container from a reservoir internal to the electronic vapor device. 7. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, further comprising a memory device coupled to the processor, wherein the processor is configured to receive a user identifier and to store the user identifier in the memory device. 8. The electronic vapor device of claim 7, wherein the processor is further configured to generate data indicating a quantity of the vaporizable material consumed by the vaporizer component in a defined period of time, and to save the data in the memory device. 9. The electronic vapor device of claim 7, further comprising a sensor positioned downstream of the vaporizer component, wherein the processor is further configured to generate the data at least in part by interpreting a signal from the sensor correlated to a quantity of vapor emitted by the vaporizer component. 10. The electronic vapor device of claim 9, further comprising a sensor positioned upstream of the vaporizer component, wherein the processor is further configured to generate the data at least in part by interpreting a signal from the sensor correlated to at least one of: an amount of the vaporizable material remaining in the first container, or an amount of the vaporizable material passed from the first container to the vaporizer component. 11. The electronic vapor device of claim 8, wherein the processor is further configured to transmit the data in association with the user identifier to the remote server via the network access device. 12. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, further comprising an input device, coupled to the processor, configured to receive a request for replenishing the vaporizable material. 13. The electronic vapor device of claim 7, wherein the processor is further configured to transmit the request in association with the user identifier to the remote server via the network access device. 14. The electronic vapor device of claim 13, wherein the processor is further configured to facilitate a commercial transaction for replenishing the vaporizable material, at least in part by one of more of: transmitting a payment authorization associated with the 

 request via the network access device to the remote server and receiving a proof-of-payment certificate via the network access device from the remote server. 15. The electronic vapor device of claim 1, further comprising: a second container for storing a non-vaporizable material; a dispenser coupled to the second container, configured for dispensing the non- vaporizable material; and wherein the processor is coupled to the dispenser and configured to control the dispenser in response to the disposition signal. 16. The electronic vapor device of claim 15, wherein the disposition signal comprises one or more of, a command to dispense the non-vaporizable material, a command to disable the dispenser, a command to enable the dispenser, a command to place the dispenser in an on-demand state, a command to mix the non-vaporizable material with another non-vaporizable material, a command to mix the vaporizable material with the non- vaporizable material, a command to replenish the non-vaporizable material, a command to deplete the non-vaporizable material without dispensing the non-vaporizable material. 17. A method comprising: receiving, by an electronic vapor device, a disposition signal from a remote server; determining, by the electronic vapor device, a command based on the disposition signal; and executing the command, by the electronic vapor device, wherein executing the command affects a disposition of a vaporizable material. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the disposition signal is received via one or more of, Wi-Fi, TCP/IP, Ethernet, UDP, WAP, Bluetooth, Near Field Communication (NFC), Z-wave, LPWAP, Telnet, HTTP, HTTPs, GSM, CDMA, or LTE. 19. The method of claim 17, wherein the command comprises one or more of a command to vaporize the vaporizable material, a command to disable the vaporizer component, a command to enable the vaporizer component, a command to place the vaporizer component in an on-demand state, a command to mix the vaporizable material with 

 another vaporizable material, a command to mix the vaporizable material with a non- vaporizable material, a command to replenish the vaporizable material, a command to deplete the vaporizable material without vaporizing the vaporizable material. 20. The method of claim 17, wherein executing the command comprises activating a disposition control mechanism operatively interposed between a vaporizer component and a container of the electronic vapor device.
